points.text: CRISPR Therapeutics reported Q1 2026 revenue of $43 million driven by CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) sales, with over 500 patients globally initiating treatment for sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals completed U.S. regulatory submission for CASGEVY in children ages 5 to 11 with sickle cell disease or beta thalassemia, receiving an FDA Commissioner's National Priority Voucher for accelerated review.
CRISPR Therapeutics expanded zugo-cel into new autoimmune indications and advanced multiple in vivo liver-directed programs toward the clinic using lipid nanoparticle-mediated delivery.
Intellia Therapeutics awarded inducement grants of 208,850 restricted stock units to 43 new employees under its 2024 Inducement Plan to support CRISPR gene editing operations.
Editas Medicine presented preclinical data on EDIT-401, an in vivo CRISPR gene editing therapy, demonstrating LDLR protein upregulation and LDL-C reduction for hyperlipidemia treatment.

CRISPR Therapeutics reported Q1 2026 financial results while advancing its pipeline of CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing medicines, including the approved CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) for sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia.

CRISPR Therapeutics is expanding its gene editing toolkit via SyNTase™, a proprietary platform designed for precise, efficient, and scalable gene correction across hemoglobinopathies, cardiovascular, autoimmune, oncology, and rare disease indications.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing — Clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company — Management scheduled for fireside chats at Bank of America, RBC Capital Markets, and Jefferies Global Healthcare Conferences in May/June 2026.
CRISPR Therapeutics reported Q1 2026 revenue of $43 million driven by CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) commercial momentum in sickle cell disease and beta thalassemia.

Vertex completed U.S. regulatory submission for CASGEVY in children ages 5 to 11 with sickle cell disease or transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, receiving an FDA Commissioner's National Priority Voucher.
Over 500 patients globally have initiated CASGEVY treatment as of Q1 2026, with 90% of U.S. patients having reimbursed access.
CRISPR Therapeutics is advancing in vivo hematopoietic stem cell editing programs using lipid nanoparticle (LNP)-mediated delivery to expand addressable populations for SCD and TDT.

Caribou Biosciences received FDA Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation for CB-011, an allogeneic anti-BCMA CRISPR-engineered CAR-T cell therapy for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma.

CB-011 Phase 1 CaMMouflage trial dose escalation data (N=12 BCMA-naïve) showed 92% overall response rate, 75% ≥ complete response rate, and 91% minimal residual disease negativity at the 450x10^6 cell dose.
Intellia Therapeutics awarded inducement grants of 208,850 restricted stock units (RSUs) to 43 new employees under its 2024 Inducement Plan to leverage CRISPR gene editing technologies.
Editas Medicine presented new preclinical data on EDIT-401, an in vivo CRISPR gene editing therapy targeting hyperlipidemia, at the ASGCT 2026 Annual Meeting and EAS Congress.
CRISPR Therapeutics reported Q1 2026 financial results and confirmed management presentation at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Global Healthcare Conference on May 12, 2026.

CRISPR Therapeutics advanced its pipeline with CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel), the first approved CRISPR-based therapy for sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, while expanding its SyNTase gene correction platform.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing — Clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company — Management scheduled for fireside chats at Bank of America (May 12), RBC Capital Markets (May 20), and Jefferies (June 3) 2026 conferences.
CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing is cited by Khyati Raina, a postdoctoral fellow at Albert Einstein College of Medicine, as a landmark discovery shaping her work in stem cell biology and organoid technology.
Khyati Raina (Albert Einstein College of Medicine) cites CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing as a landmark discovery shaping her work in stem cell biology and organoid technology.

CRISPR Therapeutics — CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Sickle Cell Disease/Beta Thalassemia — Q1 2026 revenue $43M; >500 patients treated; Vertex submitted pediatric indication (ages 5-11) with FDA Priority Voucher.
CRISPR Therapeutics — zugo-cel — Autoimmune Indications — Expanded clinical development into new autoimmune indications in Q1 2026.
CRISPR Therapeutics — CTX310 — Angiopoietin-related indications — In vivo liver-directed program utilizing LNP-mediated delivery advanced toward clinical entry.
Intellia Therapeutics — Inducement Grants — Corporate Operations — Awarded 208,850 RSUs to 43 new hires under 2024 Inducement Plan to advance CRISPR pipeline; grants vest annually over three years.

Editas Medicine — EDIT-401 — Hyperlipidemia — Presented new preclinical data at ASGCT 2026 and EAS Congress showing in vivo CRISPR-mediated LDLR upregulation and LDL-C reduction in mice and non-human primates.

CRISPR Therapeutics — CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Sickle Cell Disease & Transfusion-Dependent Beta Thalassemia — World's first CRISPR-based therapy approved; company advancing broad pipeline via SyNTase™ platform.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing platform — Severe disease treatments — Announced participation in three major investor conferences (Bank of America, RBC, Jefferies) to discuss curative treatment pipeline.

CRISPR Therapeutics — CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Sickle Cell Disease / Beta Thalassemia — Q1 2026 revenue $43M; Vertex submitted pediatric indication (ages 5-11) to FDA with Priority Voucher; >500 patients treated globally.
CRISPR Therapeutics — CTX310 — Angiopoietin-receptor targeting — In vivo liver-directed program advancing via LNP-mediated delivery platform.
CRISPR Therapeutics — zugo-cel — Autoimmune indications — Expanded into new autoimmune indications per Q1 2026 business update.
Caribou Biosciences — CB-011 — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — FDA granted RMAT designation based on Phase 1 data showing 92% ORR and 75% ≥CR in BCMA-naïve cohort; dose expansion ongoing.
Intellia Therapeutics — Inducement Grants — Corporate Governance — Awarded 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees under 2024 Inducement Plan to support CRISPR-based drug development.
Editas Medicine — EDIT-401 — Hyperlipidemia — Presented preclinical data at ASGCT 2026 demonstrating in vivo CRISPR upregulation of LDLR protein to lower LDL-C in mice and non-human primates.

CRISPR Therapeutics — CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Sickle Cell Disease & Transfusion-Dependent Beta Thalassemia — Approved CRISPR/Cas9-based ex vivo gene editing therapy; commercialization ongoing with Vertex Pharmaceuticals.

CRISPR Therapeutics — SyNTase — Broad pipeline (hemoglobinopathies, cardiovascular, oncology) — Proprietary gene correction platform designed for precise, efficient, and scalable editing; expanding toolkit beyond Cas9.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing platform — Severe disease treatments — Announced participation in three major investor conferences in May/June 2026 to discuss clinical progress and commercialization strategy.
CRISPR-Cas9 — Gene Editing — General Research — Identified by postdoctoral fellow Khyati Raina as a transformative technology enabling cellular reprogramming alongside iPSCs.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Commercial scaling of ex vivo gene editing and advancement of in vivo platforms — CASGEVY + zugo-cel + CTX310 — Q1 2026 revenue $43M; >500 patients treated; strengthened balance sheet; multiple 2026 milestones.
Vertex Pharmaceuticals — Pediatric expansion of CRISPR-based therapy — CASGEVY — Completed U.S. regulatory submission for ages 5-11; secured FDA Priority Voucher for accelerated review.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization — NTLA pipeline — Awarded 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees in May 2026 to support clinical-stage CRISPR programs.

Editas Medicine — In vivo CRISPR therapy development — EDIT-401 (hyperlipidemia) — Presented five abstracts at ASGCT 2026, TIDES USA 2026, and EAS Congress 2026 demonstrating durable LDL-C lowering via CRISPR.

CRISPR Therapeutics — CRISPR/Cas9 and SyNTase™ gene editing commercialization — CASGEVY + diversified pipeline (hemoglobinopathies, oncology, autoimmune) — Q1 2026 financial results reported; strategic collaboration with Vertex Pharmaceuticals ongoing.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization — Potentially curative treatments for severe diseases — Leading clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company leveraging CRISPR technology to reset medical standards.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Commercial scale-up of ex vivo gene editing and advancement of in vivo LNP platform — CASGEVY + CTX310 + zugo-cel — Q1 2026 revenue $43M; >500 patients treated; pediatric submission filed; multiple in vivo programs advancing.

Caribou Biosciences — CRISPR-based allogeneic CAR-T commercialization — CB-011 (anti-BCMA) — RMAT designation secured; Phase 1 CaMMouflage trial enrolling BCMA-naïve and BCMA-exposed cohorts with initial data expected in 2026.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization — NTLA pipeline — Awarded 208,850 RSUs to 43 new hires in May 2026 to accelerate clinical-stage CRISPR programs.
Editas Medicine — In vivo CRISPR therapy for cardiovascular disease — EDIT-401 — Presented oral and poster data at ASGCT 2026 and EAS Congress 2026 on LDLR upregulation mechanisms.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Commercialization of first-in-class CRISPR therapies and pipeline expansion — CASGEVY + SyNTase platform — Q1 2026 financial results reported; strategic collaboration with Vertex Pharmaceuticals active.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR-based curative treatments — NTLA (Nasdaq) — Leading clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company leveraging CRISPR gene editing to treat root causes of severe diseases; management engaging investors via upcoming conference circuit.

CASGEVY — Mechanism: Ex vivo CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing of BCL11A enhancer in hematopoietic stem cells — Phase: Approved/Commercial — Population: Severe SCD or TDT patients ≥12 years (expanding to 5-11) — Endpoints: Sustained transfusion independence or resolution of severe vaso-occlusive crises; >500 patients treated globally.

CTX310 — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing via lipid nanoparticle (LNP) delivery targeting liver cells — Phase: Pre-clinical/IND-enabling — Population: Angiopoietin-related indications — Status: Advanced toward clinic entry.

EDIT-401 — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing to disrupt gene repressor element and upregulate LDLR protein — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Mice and non-human primates — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Meaningful reduction in LDL-C and normalization of disease-associated biomarkers.

CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing targeting BCL11A enhancer — Phase: Approved (commercial) — Population: Sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia — Status: First CRISPR-based therapy approved globally.

SyNTase™ — Mechanism: Proprietary CRISPR-based gene correction platform enabling precise, efficient, scalable editing — Phase: Preclinical/Development — Population: Hemoglobinopathies, cardiovascular, autoimmune, oncology, rare diseases — Status: Expanding gene editing toolkit.

Intellia Therapeutics pipeline — Mechanism: CRISPR gene editing — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Patients with severe diseases — Objective: Develop and commercialize potentially curative treatments targeting root causes of disease.

CASGEVY — Mechanism: Ex vivo CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing of BCL11A enhancer in hematopoietic stem cells — Phase: Approved (commercial) — Population: Patients 12+ with severe SCD or TDT (pediatric 5-11 submission pending) — Endpoints: Commercial uptake >500 patients; 90% U.S. reimbursement coverage.

CTX310 — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing targeting angiopoietin-receptor via lipid nanoparticle (LNP) delivery — Phase: Pre-clinical/IND-enabling (implied by 'advancing toward clinic') — Population: Liver-directed indications — Status: Advancing diversified portfolio.

CB-011 — Mechanism: CRISPR-engineered allogeneic anti-BCMA CAR-T — Phase: 1 (CaMMouflage) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (BCMA-naïve and BCMA-exposed) — Comparator: None (single-arm) — Results: 92% ORR, 75% ≥CR, 91% MRD negativity at 450x10^6 dose; manageable safety profile with no GVHD observed.

EDIT-401 — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR disruption of gene repressor element to upregulate LDLR — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Mice and non-human primates — Results: Durable LDL-C reduction and normalization of disease-associated biomarkers.

CASGEVY (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated ex vivo gene editing targeting BCL11A enhancer — Phase: Approved (commercial) — Population: Sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia — Comparator: Standard of care — Status: World's first CRISPR-based therapy approved.

SyNTase — Mechanism: Novel proprietary gene correction platform enabling precise editing — Phase: Preclinical/Early clinical (implied by 'advancing pipeline') — Population: Hemoglobinopathies, cardiovascular, autoimmune, oncology, rare diseases — Status: Expanding gene editing toolkit beyond Cas9.

Intellia Therapeutics pipeline — Mechanism: CRISPR gene editing — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Patients with severe diseases — Objective: Develop potentially curative treatments by resetting disease root causes.

CRISPR-Cas9 — Mechanism: Gene editing — Context: Cited as a transformative technology alongside iPSCs by researcher Khyati Raina at Albert Einstein College of Medicine — Status: Referenced as a foundational milestone in scientific research.

CRISPR-Cas9 — Mechanism: Gene editing — Phase: Research tool — Population: N/A — Results: Cited as a foundational technology for reprogramming fully developed cells (e.g., skin fibroblasts) in organoid research.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Commercial reimbursement expansion — CASGEVY — Secured pricing agreement in Germany; 90% U.S. patient access reimbursed as of year-end 2025; coverage active in UK, Italy, Austria, Denmark, Luxembourg, KSA, Bahrain, UAE, Kuwait.
Intellia Therapeutics — Equity compensation — Corporate governance — Granted 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees under 2024 Inducement Plan approved by compensation committee per Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4).
CRISPR Therapeutics — Strategic collaboration expansion — Vertex Pharmaceuticals partnership — Ongoing joint development and commercialization of CRISPR-based therapies including CASGEVY.

Intellia Therapeutics — Investor relations strategy — CRISPR pipeline communication — Scheduled management appearances at Bank of America (May 12), RBC Capital Markets (May 20), and Jefferies (June 3) conferences.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Commercial expansion — CASGEVY — Secured pricing agreement in Germany; reimbursement active in U.S., U.K., Italy, Austria, Denmark, Luxembourg, KSA, Bahrain, UAE, Kuwait; Q1 2026 revenue $43M.

Caribou Biosciences — Strategic conference participation — CB-011 pipeline — Scheduled to present data at BofA Securities 2026 Health Care Conference to advance investor visibility on CRISPR CAR-T program.
Intellia Therapeutics — Equity compensation — 2024 Inducement Plan — Granted 208,850 RSUs vesting over three years to 43 new employees as material inducement for CRISPR-focused roles.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Strategic collaboration and commercialization — CASGEVY (exa-cel) — Partnership with Vertex Pharmaceuticals continues for global commercialization of the approved CRISPR therapy.

Intellia Therapeutics — Investor relations strategy — CRISPR gene editing programs — Scheduled management appearances at Bank of America, RBC Capital Markets, and Jefferies healthcare conferences in May/June 2026 to update market on CRISPR pipeline progress.
